raw_question: Among adults with type 2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease who are already receiving an SGLT2 inhibitor, how could a research cohort be structured to evaluate a configurable clinical outcome during follow-up?

pico.population: Adults 18 years or older with type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) and chronic kidney disease (CKD stages 1–5, excluding ESRD) with at least one SGLT2 inhibitor prescription in the identification window
pico.population_detail: Prevalent user design: patients who have an SGLT2 prescription in a defined identification window (rather than first-ever prescription). Index date = first qualifying SGLT2 prescription within the window. Minimum 12-month baseline lookback required.
pico.intervention: Ongoing SGLT2 inhibitor exposure (prevalent use; patients may have been on therapy before the identification window opens)
pico.comparator: Configurable at analysis time — options include: (a) no comparator (single-arm characterization), (b) DPP-4 inhibitor class, (c) GLP-1 receptor agonist class, (d) no add-on therapy. Comparator specification is required before outcomes analysis.
outcomes: Configurable primary outcome (user-specified at run time — see outcome_options in structured_json)
eGFR-based CKD progression (default example: sustained 40% decline in eGFR from baseline, confirmed at two consecutive measurements)
MACE — major adverse cardiovascular events (non-fatal MI, non-fatal stroke, cardiovascular death)
Hospitalization for heart failure (primary diagnosis on inpatient claim or Encounter)
All-cause mortality (where data source provides death records)
eGFR slope (continuous outcome: mean annual rate of eGFR change during follow-up)
pico.timeframe: Configurable follow-up period (default: 12 months from index date); configurable censoring rules (treatment discontinuation, data end, competing events). Maximum follow-up capped at data source coverage.
pico.study_intent: Study design template for evaluating configurable outcomes in a prevalent SGLT2 inhibitor user cohort — demonstrates how cohort definition parameters (comparator, outcome, follow-up, censoring) interact

ambiguity_flags.description: The primary clinical outcome is not defined — this is intentionally left configurable. Without a pre-specified primary outcome, inference is descriptive only and not hypothesis-testing
ambiguity_flags.suggested_clarification: Which outcome is the primary endpoint for this analysis? Select one from: CKD progression (eGFR decline), hospitalization for heart failure, MACE, all-cause mortality, or a custom outcome.

ambiguity_flags.description: The comparator group is configurable, which affects study design (active comparator vs. no comparator) and the estimand. Each choice implies different confounding structures.
ambiguity_flags.suggested_clarification: Is this a single-arm characterization or a comparative effectiveness study? If comparative, which drug class is the active comparator?

ambiguity_flags.description: Exposure continuity definition is not specified — should patients be censored at treatment discontinuation, or is intention-to-treat follow-up used? Gap-tolerance (e.g., 30-day supply gap allowed) must be defined.
ambiguity_flags.suggested_clarification: How is SGLT2 inhibitor exposure continuity defined? What gap in days supply triggers treatment discontinuation and censoring?

ambiguity_flags.description: Prevalent user design introduces immortal time bias if not handled correctly. The index date definition must align with when exposure status is ascertained.
ambiguity_flags.suggested_clarification: How is the identification window defined? Should there be a minimum SGLT2 exposure duration before the index date to ensure stable exposure?

clarifying_questions: Which clinical outcome is the pre-specified primary endpoint: CKD progression (eGFR decline), heart failure hospitalization, MACE, or another outcome?
Is this a single-arm descriptive study or a comparative effectiveness study requiring an active comparator group?
If comparative: which drug class serves as the active comparator (DPP-4 inhibitors, GLP-1 agonists, or sulfonylureas)?
How should SGLT2 exposure continuity be defined — days supply with a permissible gap (e.g., 30 days), or any prescription in a rolling window?
What is the minimum follow-up duration required for a patient to contribute to the outcome analysis?
How should censoring be handled for competing events (e.g., eGFR=0 for cardiovascular outcomes, or death for kidney outcomes)?

kidney.label: CKD progression
kidney.definition: Sustained 40% decline in eGFR from baseline confirmed at two consecutive measurements at least 90 days apart, or initiation of renal replacement therapy

cardiovascular.label: MACE
cardiovascular.definition: Composite of non-fatal myocardial infarction (ICD-10-CM I21.*), non-fatal ischemic stroke (I63.*), or cardiovascular death

heart_failure.label: Hospitalization for heart failure
heart_failure.definition: Inpatient admission with primary diagnosis ICD-10-CM I50.* (heart failure)

mortality.label: All-cause mortality
mortality.definition: Death from any cause during follow-up (requires death date in data source)

censoring_rules: Treatment discontinuation (configurable gap tolerance, default 30 days)
Initiation of competing therapy (if active comparator design)
structured_json.estimand_note: Estimand must be pre-specified before analysis: treatment policy, hypothetical, or while-on-treatment. This template does not impose an estimand — researcher must define.
